title: Dependency Management Policy
body:
# Dependency Management Policy

This policy defines how project dependencies are approved, reviewed, and remediated. All third-party and open-source libraries must be scanned for known vulnerabilities using the organization's approved scanning tools.

## Severity-to-Priority Mapping

| Severity   | Ticket Priority | Category  | Tags                   |
|------------|-----------------|-----------|------------------------|
| Critical   | high           | security  | urgent, dependency     |
| High       | high           | security  | urgent, dependency     |
| Medium     | medium         | security  | dependency             |
| Low        | low            | security  | dependency             |

When a vulnerability is identified, create a ticket in the ticketing system and assign priority, category, and tags according to the table above. The remediation must follow the standard vulnerability response process.

title: Third-Party Library Approval Process
body: All new third-party libraries must be approved by the architecture team. Submit a request with the library name, version, and intended use. The security team will perform a preliminary vulnerability assessment before approval is granted.

title: Security Vulnerability Response Guide
body: When a vulnerability is reported, assign an owner, determine severity, and create a ticket following the Dependency Management Policy (KB-101). Critical and High findings must be remediated within 48 hours. All remediation activities must be documented in the tracking ticket.
